Oracle-native Doom · build review

checking stack…
Oracle Free 23ai · ORDS · 2 CPU / 2 GiB

Current milestone

30 FPS playability gate
Exact SQL frames · active

P12.0 is pulled ahead of parity. P8.1 remains safely preserved at tic 1430 with 46 health and 9 kills while renderer work targets unique moving frames at no more than 33.3 ms.

Canonical acceptance

P0–P7 — complete
renderer, simulation, combat, history, audio, mutation and browser gates

T8.1 — tic 1,019
20 HP · 3 armor · 7 kills · 10 items · alive

static healthORDS

Live implementation status · July 15, 2026

Route: preserved behind the playability gate; public DOOM_TIC_TX.APPLY_BATCH only; lift 1 completed and the tic-1430 checkpoint remains committed. Next after P12.0: lift 2, blue key, exit, repeatability, and reviewed milestone frames.

Recent integration fixes now have focused regressions: finite-portal boundary enforcement, transactional MOBJ reference cleanup, save/load command-lineage isolation, and rising-floor actor carry. No noclip, teleport, direct simulation calls, or evaluator state mutation is accepted as route evidence.

Performance: pulled-forward T12.0 reduced exact warm clean NEW_GAME from 121.79 s to 7.63 s. A clean-bootstrap first frame is 8.89 s, one moving turn is 8.71 s, and four forward tics are 11.35 s. All retain exact state/frame hashes and payload bytes; this is ≈16× faster, but still only ≈0.115 moving FPS and not interactively playable.

Approved visual checkpoint · three poses

Reviewed pose

loading reviewed identity…

—
indexed PNG SHA-256

Each option is an independently decoded, deterministic indexed PNG made from the captured SQL frame and PLAYPAL rows. All three passed direct visual inspection and byte-level SQL/RLE/RGBA/PNG agreement.

Verified game output · E1M1 portal renderer

Frame diagnostic

loading database frame…

—
canonical pixels

—
planes / portal pieces / solid walls

—
database frame SHA-256

This artifact is generated by querying DOOM_R2_PIXELS at tic 17 and the relational PLAYPAL rows. Portal windows, sector planes, sky, animation, texture offsets, pegging, and lighting are selected in Oracle; the browser only applies the palette.

Approved game output · masked walls + world sprites

Reviewed R2 pose

loading reviewed masked identity…

—
selected overlay pixels

—
masked walls / sprite sources

—
indexed PNG SHA-256

These are actual 64,000-pixel Oracle frames composed from DOOM_R2_PIXELS and the deterministic winners from DOOM_R2_MASKED_PIXELS. Transparent holes expose the world beneath; the browser displays the reviewed PNG bytes unchanged.

Verified game output · R1 ray fan

Spawn diagnostic

loading database artifact…

—
camera columns

—
analytic intersections

—
nearest linedefs

Every amber ray and red endpoint is emitted by Oracle's reviewed T4.1 SQL macros at the actual E1M1 player-one spawn. This is geometry diagnostics, not a mocked game frame.

Build-review surface only · no credentials or evaluator data are exposed to the browser.
